observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with the DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 and Administrative Order No. 26 and demand refund to the payees and collect the same.  Otherwise, the amount disbursed will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management explained that the disbursement was supported by a local ordinance approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan. Further, Management justified that it was in line with its exercise of its fiscal autonomy considering the needs during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason:
DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 clearly indicates that an agency can only pay Hazard Pay to those who are occupying regular, contractual or casual positions, or are workers engaged through COS, JO, or other similar work arrangements. Barangay officials and employees are not employees of the Municipality and are employed separately by their respective barangay which also have their own separate budget as
mentioned in Section 5 of Administrative Order No. 26 and which are also subject to their own budgetary limitation with regard to hazard pay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant follow the required format, and issue Confirmation, and Demand Letters to the specific persons liable to immediately settle the cash advances either by returning the money advanced if unspent, or by the presentation of required supporting documents. 

We also recommended that a separate filing system be made for the granting of cash advance and liquidation thereof.  Such supporting documents for the granting and liquidation of cash advances, including the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect should be submitted to the Auditor. Failure on their part to comply with the submission of the liquidations shall be a ground for disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | The Municipal Accountant had submitted updated status of unliquidated cash advances with the specific details.
observations.reason:
There is no proof submitted as to the exhaustion of remedies to collect/liquidate those which are still outstanding.

Reiterated in Part II of this report. [AOM No. 2022-004(2021)]

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide a reasonable basis for why such a substantial amount of disbursements were made through reimbursements basis. Moreover, we recommended that management adhere to the regulations requiring payments to be made in checks drawn on duly approved vouchers and made payable directly to the creditor to ensure that disbursements are properly supported and that the required withholding tax has been imposed. Adherence to the provisions of Section 93 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 is enjoined, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020 | Management explained that disbursements were made through reimbursements due to the extreme urgency of providing food to residents staying in a quarantine facility. To ensure timely provision of meals, Management had decided to buy raw food supplies and materials from the public market vendors. Considering the situation, it has caused the departure of the usual process of disbursement of the Municipality on matters relative to safety and protection from/against Covid-19. 

Significantly, the disbursement is not disadvantageous to the government because of the substantial amount of cost savings realized arising thereto. Tabulation will be submitted the soonest time possible.
observations.reason:
There is no submission of the required documents to substantiate the justification of Management.  

During the current year, the practice is still ongoing and AOM No. 2022-010(2021) was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the required supporting documents with complete data, otherwise, the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and shall hold all those who authorized the payment as jointly and severally liable for the amount disbursed. Adherence to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 is enjoined, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020 | Management explained that the distribution list is not applicable because of the prohibition by the health department on direct contact with Covid-19 patients. Only one person was allowed then, and that was the attendant from the monitoring team who was tasked to receive the meals and distribute the same to the patients. Patients are likewise not permitted to sign and have contact with documents due to strict health protocols. 

Management had promised to submit the estimate on food preparation and purchases in relation to the estimated number of beneficiaries.
observations.reason:
There is no submission of the required documents to substantiate the justification of management.  

During the current year, the practice is still ongoing and AOM No. 2022-010(2021) was issued.

There is no proof of any savings on the matter absent cost-benefit analysis and there is also no proper control on the expenditures considering that it is not even duly supported by the estimate on food preparation and purchases in relation to the estimated number of beneficiaries, nor is it supported by any list of valid recipients who signify that they received such benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as of December 31, 2020 together with the necessary supporting documents and henceforth, adhere to the provisions and reportorial requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that aids and donations received by the Municipality are properly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management explained that the Municipality is currently going through its records relative to the donations-in-kind received and will submit the corresponding documents when done.
observations.reason: There is no submission of the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management make a written explanation/justification on why the liquidated damages have not been computed, demanded and paid and why the retention money was not deducted from the contractor’s payment for evaluation by this Commission, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.   

We also recommended that the management demand from the contractor and collect the amount for the liquidated damages and retention money, otherwise responsible persons should be held liable for the said deficiencies for neglect of duty in the performance of their duties and for giving undue warranted benefits to a private party in accordance with Anti-Graft and Corrupt Practices Act (RA 3019).

We too recommended that the Municipal Engineer monitor the contract date and the expected completion date of the project and notify the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer if there are delays in the project implementation in order to compute for liquidated damages before final payment. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer properly review the supporting documents of construction projects before payment is made and make sure that retention money is withheld, and liquidation damages are demanded and paid for in a timely manner to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020 | Management explained that the contract extension were due to the following:

Extension No. 1 – was issued on April 22, 2019 due to preliminary revision of top elevation to preserve the heritage view of the church granting 110 days of time extension.

Extension No. 2 – was issued on August 30, 2019 due to slowdown of work pending the approval of the tree cutting permit application granting 60 days of time extension.

Extension No. 3 – was issued on October 18, 2019 due to the final revision of top elevation granting 60 days of time extension.
observations.reason:
The reasons provided by Management are not valid aside from the fact that it is belatedly submitted. The reasons for request for contract extension is not among the specific conditions allowed under the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects as it is foreseeable and can be considered even before the start of the project through detailed engineering.
The request for extension is not valid as it was not properly supported and was only belatedly attached after seven months of delayed completion wherein no liquidated

damages has been computed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality comply with Paragraphs 6.1.7 and 6.1.8 of the COA/DBM/DILG/ Governance Commission for GOCCs/Department of National Defense Joint Circular by providing the Minutes of the Meeting evidencing 2/3 votes of the Peace and Order Council approving the POP and release of the CF, duly signed by those present. Moreover, the related Resolution should be signed by the Peace and Order Council.

CY 2018 – ₱5,775,000.00
CY 2019 – ₱1,500,000.00
 Total      – ₱7,275,000.00
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2019
AAR CY 2018 | The Management will provide the actual minutes of the meeting. Moreover, it provides that the minutes were taken but it was overlooked that the related document was a part of the supporting document required but the agency was able to provide minutes after the request.
observations.reason: There were no Minutes of Meeting provided until now.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality comply with the requirements under Paragraphs 4.4.1 and 4.4.2 of the Joint DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2017-3, Policy Guidelines and Procedures in the Implementation of the FY 2017 Local Government Support Fund-Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (LGSF- ADM) Program in order to ensure that the project will be sustainable in the long run. 
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipality devise plans and policies as to the operations, maintenance and repairs of the Potable Water Project so it can function properly for the years to come in accordance with the estimated useful life of the infrastructure asset.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2019 | Management informed that documentary requirements already provided to DILG. Moreover, it states that the drilling site was moved. MIWASSCO already applied for the permit/certification and waiting for the documents to be available.

Management will submit the lacking documents once available:

1. ECC
2. NWRB Permit
3. MOA with MIWASSCO
observations.reason: There is no document submitted to substantiate compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with Budget Circular No. 2018-5. Further, officials and employees should be made to refund the amount received.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2018 | Management informed that the policy guidelines on conditions for the grant of CNA Incentive “LGU should have accomplished as of September 30, 2018, at least an average of 70% of all the targets under their programs/activities/
projects approved in the LGU budget for FY 2018.” 

Based on its rationale, purpose and on the above-quoted policy guideline, the 70% utilization or accomplishment should therefore be based on the PPAs and not on their budget appropriation. And considering that the Municipality accomplished 70% of its PPAs, the CNA incentives were properly and legally released to its employees.
observations.reason:
There were no documents that were submitted to substantiate such claims.

Also, based on the audit of CY 2019, 2020 and 2021, the Municipality of Minglanilla granted the same privilege without any supporting documents that would substantiate such claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property/ special education tax collection. The Municipality should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through a levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2012 | Management informed that they would implement the necessary steps or program to demand the taxpayers of delinquent payees through sending of a notice of delinquency and record or notify the public.

However, they also informed that they cannot provide the required data as they have not yet availed of any software or program that would help them determine RPT delinquency at any given time. As of the moment, the LGU is still using the manual basis of recording.
observations.reason: Management has not yet implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should take the initiative that all government vehicles should be marked appropriately. Moreover, proper documentation must be prepared to support usage and consumption of gasoline, oil and lubricants in compliance with COA Circular No. 75-6.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2011 | Management informed that all vehicles are already scheduled for inspection and proper making by paint. The two vehicles now bear permanent government plates. Signature of both driver and passengers and monthly report of official travel are now being complied with.

Management informed that the vehicles without “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” were left unnoticed that the stickers were already missing. The vehicles missing permanent government plates was due to the delay/ unavailability of the plate numbers.
observations.reason: There are no supporting documents to support the action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials should prioritize the implementation of projects funded out of Financial Assistance so that expected benefits due to intended recipients be timely achieved or delivered.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2010 | Management informed that upon tracing by the Municipal Accountant in the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Municipality incorporated in the PPSAS Accounting System, there were no records in relation to the financial assistance from Senator Angara for the year 2010 leading to a judgment that either the FA itself had already been fully utilized or if in the case of non-utilization, the said fund has already been returned where it was.
observations.reason: The Audit Team has not received any liquidation of the said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality should spend and utilize its resources with efficiency and economy to prevent, if not, minimize unnecessary and excessive expenses and observe strictly Sections 163 and 165 of the GAAM and COA circular 85-55A.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2010 | Management said that they do believe that the increase in the disbursements for expenses for Sugat Kabanhawan Festival is reasonable in the sense that the festival has always been a tradition of Minglanilla and this festivity serves not only the inhabitants of Minglanilla but all around Cebu itself. Given the increase in demand yearly, it is only reasonable that there be an increase in the expenses as well. Improving the Sugat Kabanhawan Festival is also one way of promoting Minglanilla as a whole.
observations.reason: We have observed a steady increase in the Municipality’s budgeted expenses related to the annual Sugat/Kabanhawan Festival.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the depository bank to furnish them with the bank statements and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Savings Account and DBP Trust Account. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the municipal books.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and demand liquidation of these cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to conduct a physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year. Further, direct the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, for each category of PPE in the form as prescribed in the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to take necessary actions in addressing the issues on pending land titling. Likewise, submit the scope of work of land titling for budget allocation and facilitate the transfer of title of the unregistered parcels of land to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management verify the actual physical status of the Infrastructure Projects still recorded in the CIP Accounts and direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare appropriate entries to reclassify all completed projects to their appropriate property, plant and equipment accounts in accordance with pertinent rules and regulations.
We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to record the following proposed Journal Entries:

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the amounts granted under Subsidy to Other LGUs to Due from LGUs account and demand submission of liquidation reports to ascertain if utilizations are made in accordance with the purpose for which it was granted before taking up the adjustments in the books. Henceforth, ensure that the documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 as amended by COA Circular No. 2017-002 shall be complied with before any funds shall be released by the Municipality to the barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts to correct the balances of the subject accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly Trial Balance, schedule of Subsidiary Ledger Balances and Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations not later than the 20th day after the end of the month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to review the disbursement process to address the reason/s for the delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers and other reports.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Engineer, agreed to submit the “As-Built Plans” of the subject completed projects to substantiate its final payment.  We also recommended that and Management agreed to comply with Section 62.2.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 requiring the issuance of a Certificate of Final Acceptance one year from project completion.

We recommended that Management:
submit an amended LDRRMFIP that includes the subject expenditures charged against the LDRRMF and as incorporated in the revised Annual Investment Program (AIP); and
henceforth, stop the practice of treating unexpended DRRMF-MOOE as continuing appropriation and transfer the same to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
instruct the concerned personnel to submit an explanation of the noted deficiencies; and
submit certified true copies of the certificates from the manufacturer that the supplier is an authorized distributor of the items procured during the Post-Qualification; and
henceforth, ensure that brand names and other details are indicated in the Purchase Order (PO), Delivery Receipt (DR) and Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to make sure that the intended specifications of the items procured are met; and the BAC to include the other appropriate licenses and permits to be required by law in the Bidding documents and subject to Post-Qualification evaluation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
require the Municipal Jail Warden to submit a utilization report together with the Roster of Inmates duly certified by the Jail Warden or a list showing the recipients of the food supplies, in support of the recorded expenses;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to set-up the Food Supplies for Distribution Account (1-04-02-010) in the books for the purchase of rice intended for the consumption of Persons Deprived of Liberty (PDL) and record the corresponding Food Supplies Expense (5-02-03-050) only upon submission of the required utilization report; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to accept only Official Receipt with the name of the Municipality as proof of the payment made.

We recommended that Management initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the mandatory vacant positions with the appropriate qualifications so that efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained.

We recommended that Management initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the mandatory and other vacant positions with the appropriate qualifications so that efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained. Further, Management is enjoined to observe the rules and regulations on designation as mandated by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the grant of cash advance is supported with complete documentary requirements under Item 1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Moreover, instruct the concerned personnel to explain the inclusion of recipients with male first names in the monetary allowance charged against the GAD fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the year-end financial statements, trial balances and bank reconciliation statements for the team’s validation, otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned personnel pursuant to Section 41 (3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020
ML CY 2019 | The Municipal Accountant has already submitted the complete year-end financial statements.
observations.reason: Management had implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO properly assess the clients and/or beneficiaries of AICS and comply with DSWD MC No. 11 Series of 2019.  Moreover, we recommended that Management provide a reasonable explanation of the deficiencies noted and submit the necessary documents required under Items VI and VII of the same Circular for evaluation of the Commission. Otherwise, the disbursement will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020 | Section 9 of Ordinance No. 23, Series of 2019 specifies the fund appropriation from Mayor’s Office as Aid to Persons in Crisis Situation for years 2019 and 2020 and as Aid to Barangay Officials and Workers for years 2021 and onwards. Contrary to the observations of the auditor, these donations are not the same as Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS) funded by DSWD and therefore not covered by DSWD MC No. 11 Series of 2019.

The grant of financial assistance to the bereaved family of Police Staff sergeant was through Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 185 Series of 2019 to an appreciation for his years of service to the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Management had submitted an explanation that the Audit Team found acceptable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit a justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management had submitted justification that under Item 54.5 of RA 9184 warranty security is not required in emergency cases and that such warranty is already implied under Article 1547 (2) of the Civil Code. Even considering such, management responded that they would submit an undertaking signed by the suppliers as soon as it is retrieved.
observations.reason: Management had submitted a justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | The Municipal Mayor has issued a memo to ensure that documents are properly filled out and submitted within the required period.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Mayor had issued a memo to the various departments. 

However, there are still documents that are not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the submission of Inventory Reports of relief goods, medicines and medical supplies, and food supplies as of December 31, 2020 including the supporting documents for distribution and usage. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the completeness of documents and prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries. Moreover, we too recommended for the concerned officials to conduct periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of accounting and inventory records for accuracy in financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | The Municipal Accountant has already prepared the necessary adjusting entries for the inventory on-hand at year-end based on the year-end inventory report submitted by the end-user and the entries made are already incorporated in the financial statements for CY 2020
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant has yet to submit proof of adjusting entries as reflected on the CY 2020 Financial Statements. 

With regard to the summary of inventory provided by Management, it only
indicates as to how much is left but there is no proper accounting and support as to the amount recorded as an expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the required supporting documents with complete data, otherwise, the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and shall hold all those who authorized the payment as jointly and severally liable for the amount disbursed. Adherence to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 is enjoined, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | The Municipality is dependent upon the submission distribution list from the barangays. The Municipal Accountant has submitted the supporting documents. The distribution list for the first and second wave of rice, canned goods and noodles will be submitted as soon as retrieved.
observations.reason:
The transmittal letter of the boxes submitted did not indicate the specific
disbursement vouchers per box despite follow-up. Review of the documents submitted did not indicate to which disbursement voucher does the said liquidation pertains. There were also no liquidation report and the corresponding disbursement vouchers, except a list of people with signatures, some without specifications as to what they receive and in what quantities. 

Moreover, there is no distribution list submitted for the medicines and
other medical-related expenses, as well as the food supplies expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the corresponding approved disbursement vouchers with supporting documents pertinent to the transaction in accordance with the guidelines and documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation by this Commission, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.   Adherence to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management explained that the support for such disbursement was contained in boxes #104 and #105.
observations.reason: The Transmittal Letter only indicates Box #104 and #105 as support for welfare goods for distribution. A review of the supporting documents submitted did not include the corresponding liquidation reports and the related disbursement vouchers. What was submitted were folders with the name of different barangays. In it is a list of names and signatures. Some also did not specifically indicate the specific goods given and the quantity received. Thus, disbursement and liquidation of such funds cannot be properly determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management establish a website to promote transparency through the widest dissemination of information on the use of the funds and to avoid non-compliance with future similar requirements. We also recommend that management submit a justification/explanation for the non-posting and delay in the preparation of the BGCM monthly report on fund utilization and the status of implementation of PPAs. We too recommend that the management submit copies of the written notice from the LCE when the said reports were posted on its website to the government offices where the submission of the reports is required.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management explained that they already submitted the Monthly Report of Utilization and a written notice. Moreover, management explained that they will establish a website after finalizing the agreement with Central Visayas Information Sharing Network (CVISNet) Foundation for the website development and hosting of the Municipality’s official website.
observations.reason: A review of the documents submitted showed that only the Monthly Report of Utilization as of the month of September was submitted. The agency is in the process of establishing its own website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO properly assess the clients and/or beneficiaries of AICS and comply with DSWD MC No. 11 Series of 2019. Moreover, we recommended that Management provide a reasonable explanation of the deficiencies noted and submit the lacking documents required under Items VI and VII of the same circular for evaluation by the Commission. Otherwise, the disbursement will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | It is the position of management that since they are not receiving any funding from the DSWD, they are not covered by the said memorandum circular. The basis of their AICS grant is the memorandum of the Municipal Mayor issued on December 28, 2017, as to the documentary requirements for AICS. Moving forward the Sangguniang Bayan had passed Ordinance No. 06, Series of 2021, providing for the policies and guidelines on the grant of AICS and for other purposes.
observations.reason: Management had submitted an explanation but did not submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the corresponding approved disbursement vouchers with supporting documents pertinent to the transaction in accordance with the guidelines and documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation by this Commission, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.  Adherence to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management had submitted the liquidation of the cash advance.
observations.reason:
A review of the documents showed that it is only supported with a list of names alleged as having confirmed COVID cases as certified by the rural health unit doctor. 

As such the following should have been provided:
1. Identification Card
2. Clinical Abstract or Medical Certificate
3. Hospital Bill or prescription or laboratory results
4. Barangay Certificate

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the corresponding approved disbursement vouchers with supporting documents pertinent to the transaction in accordance with the guidelines and documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation by this Commission, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.  Adherence to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020 | Management explained that PNP, MITCOM and other government officials are included in the allocation because they were exposed to the risk of Covid-19 infection considering that they were assigned at the quarantine facility. Allowing them to buy their own meals and snacks from the private establishment, of which they still have to roam around because all or substantially all private establishments were closed, will possibly increase the risk on the personnel of those private establishments, its customers and the like. It is an uncompromising obligation of the Municipality to promote, protect, and secure the welfare and safety of its constituents. The purpose of such inclusion is to avoid any unnecessary community exposure.

Management also did not provide the names of covid patients in compliance with the Data Privacy Law.
observations.reason: Management had submitted an explanation. However, no submission was made with regard to the names of the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required documents enumerated under Paragraph 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 together with the justification as to the belated attachment to the disbursement voucher and the belated preparation and submission of such documents to the Commission on Audit. Otherwise, any disbursement pertaining to the variation order may be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management explained that there was no finalized variation order amounting to ₱2,495,521.44. This is because the variation order dated November 29, 2019 signed by Ricardo P. Tuanson, Sr., Engr. Joselito Nacario and Hon. Elanito A. Pen was subsequently canceled, invalidated and withdrawn on December 27, 2019 which explains why there was no payment. The amount stated in the canceled variation order is not even included in the annual budget. A copy of the cancellation was attached.
observations.reason: Management had submitted an explanation for further review and verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the data on Government Agencies Program Activities (GAPPAIS) for the four quarters of 2019 and 2020 in its required format for proper accountability and to enhance transparency with regard to the details of government projects. Otherwise, the Municipal Mayor suspend the payment of salaries of concerned officials as provided in Section 122 (2) of PD 1445. Strict compliance with Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445 is hereby recommended.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management informed that the records are still being retrieved to complete the data.
observations.reason:
Upon checking of the emailed document, we noticed that the form is
not completely filled out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management conduct a complete physical inventory count of properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor an inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
ML CY 2020
AAR CY 2011 | Management submitted a partial Inventory Count of Supplies for purchases starting June 2018, the month of assumption of Ederlita Cena, Supply Officer	III.

The GSO has already started to reconcile the documents being turned over to her and other documents related to the record of   PPE (PAR). The agency will be conducting its physical inventory and updating property records in the subsequent year.
observations.reason: A partial inventory of supplies has been submitted. However, it did not tie up with financial records. There is no periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records had not been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We required the immediate and timely submission of the CY 2020 disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for proper accountability. Otherwise, the Municipal Mayor should suspend the payment of salaries of concerned officials pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD 1445. Strict compliance with Section 100 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Government Accounting Manual is hereby recommended.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | The Municipal Mayor had issued a memo to all officials and employees responsible for the preparation of disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to strictly comply with the submission of these documents
observations.reason:
The transmittal letter of the boxes submitted did not indicate the specific
disbursement vouchers per box despite follow-up. Thus, the completeness of the
disbursement vouchers submitted cannot be fully verified.

A review of the documents placed inside the  submitted boxes disclosed that there are disbursement vouchers that are not  submitted and some Liquidation Reports with attached disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents are  also not submitted. The supporting documents are not properly referenced as to which Liquidation Report and Disbursement Vouchers it pertains. Thus, verification cannot be fully made since proper accounting and recording was not in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the corresponding approved disbursement vouchers with supporting documents pertinent to the transaction in accordance with the guidelines and documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for evaluation by this Commission, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued to this effect.  Adherence to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: ML CY 2020 | Management had submitted boxes of documents to support the transaction.
observations.reason:
A review of the supporting documents submitted does not specifically indicate as to which Liquidation Report it pertains. Moreover, the following documents as required under Item VIII (B) (1) and (4) of DSWD Memorandum Circular 2020-009 were not submitted:

Memorandum of Agreement signed by the DSWD Regional Director and Local Chief Executive
Accomplished simplified SAC forms
Photo of the beneficiary during actual receipt of the assistance

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipality should immediately prepare and submit the liquidation/disbursement reports and supporting documents to the grantor after the completion of projects so that Municipal Accountant can prepare adjusting journal entries to close the Due to NGAs/GOCCs/LGUs account and credit the corresponding government equity accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 | The Municipal Accountant informed that they have already liquidated most of the disbursed downloaded funds utilized by the agency. Moreover, they informed that they can only prepare the liquidation reports unless said downloaded funds have been utilized by the respective requesting departments. Some of the downloaded funds are still ongoing in their implementation phase and some of the downloaded funds are unutilized as of to date making it impossible to prepare the liquidation reports.
observations.reason: There is still an undisbursed balance as at December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency appropriate funds for the titling of the land and the Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort on titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government. The Accounting Office should set up and maintain complete accounting records of real properties and the Municipal Treasurer maintain and keep on file complete records of real properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 | Management informed that this is pending due to suspension for titling for all activities transacting friar lands under memorandum circular order of the secretary dated March 9, 2011. Moreover, it has been pending due to unpaid penalties from BIR. The planned course of action of Management is to pay the unpaid penalties
observations.reason: Management is still in the process of processing titling.

observations.recommendation: Make representation with the supplier to refund the questionable purchase of P917,000.00 otherwise the approving officers of the transaction will assume the liability that may arise thereof.
observations.managementAction: Municipal officials involved have already been dismissed from service as ordered by the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality should spend and utilize its resources with efficiency and economy to prevent, if not, minimize unnecessary and excessive expenses and observe strictly Sections 163 and 165 of the GAAM and COA circular 85-55-A.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has a budget of P4,000,000.00 for the year 2018 and P5,000,000.00 for the year 2019 for Kabanhawan.

observations.recommendation: An annual physical inventory should be conducted and updating and reconciliation of the accounting and property records must be undertaken in compliance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management still did not conduct the actual physical inventory. Property records were not updated and periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records had not been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should take the initiative that all government vehicles should be marked appropriately. Moreover, proper documentation must be prepared to support usage and consumption of gasoline, oil and lubricants in compliance with COA Circular No. 75-6 must be adhered.
observations.managementAction: No update given from the municipality. For further verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010. The Accountant should submit the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules on or before February 14 of each year.
observations.managementAction: There is still a delay in the submission of the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The CY 2018 FS was submitted March 28, 2019 or a delay of forty-two days (42).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the P31,000.00/ P27,400.00/ P13,000.00 excess amount of PEI received, otherwise all those involved who authorized the payments shall be held personally liable therefore.
A Notice of Disallowance will be issued in this regard.
observations.managementAction: The LGU filed a petition for review of COA RO7 decision denying their appeal from ND# 2014-001- (2013) dated March 18, 2014 on the excessive payment of PEI for CY 2013 of P4,592,400.00. COA RO7 Decision #2015-006 dated Nov. 10, 2015, denied the appeal of the LGU from ND#2015-001- (2014) on the disallowance of the CY 2014 PEI in the amount of P4,814,400.00. The LGU filed a petition for review of the said decision.
observations.reason: COA Decision #2017-333 dated September 27, 2017 was issued, Notice of Disallowance No. ND# 2014-001 (2013) dated March 18, 2014 on the excessive payment of PEI for the year 2013 to officials and employees of the Municipality of Minglanilla, Cebu, in the amount of P4,592,400.00, has been Lifted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert effort to clear the books of this dormant account.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant disclosed in the notes to FS that other receivables under the Trust Fund are old accounts under the KKK Proponent Loan prior to the year 1993, but no records can be found as to the individual listing of the loans.
observations.reason: Other Receivables under the Trust Fund amounted to P82,458.00 still reflected in their 2018 Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year and submit to the Accountant in compliance with Section 20, Vol. I of NGAS Manual, to ensure accurate and reliable recording of the RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts. Further, Management may avail the software of the National Computer Center.
observations.managementAction: No updates provided from the municipality.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should support the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables with duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year, in compliance with Section 20, Vol. I of NGAS Manual, to ensure accurate and reliable recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No updates provided from the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel has enough to support for their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.managementAction: No update provided by the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality revisit the Memorandum of Agreement to ensure proper implementation of said MOA so as not to give any unwarranted benefits to any private party, which may result to loss of government funds and property.
Moreover, we recommended that the New Slaughterhouse be properly recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The municipality will conduct an investigation on the matter.
observations.reason: The municipality is yet to submit report on this matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended strict compliance with Sections 32 of the Manual on the NGAS
for Local Government Units. The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact
his collections as well as all collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers
with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day to prevent
possible loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant religiously prepare the Monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statements for all accounts in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445
and to Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-11.
To facilitate the timely preparation of the BRS, we also recommended that the
Municipality make representation with the depository banks to furnish them Bank
Statements within 5 days after the end of each month as provided for in Item 3.1 of the
same COA Circular.

We requested that the Municipality should ensure compliance with the
documentary requirements in the procurement of real properties. We recommended that
the Municipality immediately facilitate the transfer of titles of the procured lots in the
name of the Municipality.

We recommended strict compliance with Budget Circular No. 2018-5. Further,
officials and employees should be made to refund the amount received.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with Paragraph 6.1.7 and 6.1.8 of
the COA/DBM/DILG/Governance Commission for GOCCs/Department of National
Defense Joint Circular by providing the Minutes of Meeting evidencing 2/3 votes of the
Peace and Order Council approving the POP and release of the CF, duly signed by those
present. Moreover, the related Resolution should be signed by the Peace and Order
Council.

We recommended that Municipality should immediately prepare and submit the
liquidation/disbursement reports and supporting documents to the grantor after the
completion of projects so that Municipal Accountant can prepare adjusting journal
entries to close the Due to NGAs/GOCCs/LGUs account and credit the corresponding
government equity accounts.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the program and activities of
the 20% Development Fund and 5% DRRM budget be implemented and optimized for
the benefit of their constituents. Furthermore, the Municipal Officials should exercise
prudence in the use of its limited cash resources to ensure that adequate funds are
maintained to finance all the intended projects of the municipal government.

We recommended that the Municipality pay their payroll through the use of ATM to promote efficiency and proper accountability. Through this, possible loss in transit and misused of funds are prevented. Moreover, it prevents payments to non-existent and unauthorized persons as it will be the bank who will validate the existence of their clients.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already used the atm for their payroll.
observations.reason: The municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We requested for the immediate submission of reports detailing the objectives of the activity and the actual accomplishment thereof, otherwise the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and all those who authorized the payments may be held jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed.
We recommended that extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses incurred by the Municipality be properly and completely supported with documentary requirements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: The municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Secretary prepare/submit the Minutes of the regular sessions/attendance sheets/actual work/attendance of the official vis-à-vis the work schedule or performance standards. SB Members/Municipals Officials/Resource Persons who were present during the Regular/Special Sessions should have signed such Minutes to ensure that what is embodied in the Minutes is what was duly agreed upon. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation before payments are made.
We recommended strict compliance with the aforementioned laws and regulations in the disbursement of government funds for the payment of honoraria and RATA. The concerned municipal officials should refrain from approving disbursements unless duly supported with complete documentation. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should see to it that all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim before payments are made to avoid suspension and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: The municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality appropriate in their Annual Budget one percent (1%) of its IRA for the implementation/strengthening of programs, projects and activities of their respective LCP. Such allocation should be duly supported with specific practical and doable plans.
Failure to implement the provision under Section 15 of RA 9344 will be sanctioned under Section 62 of the said Act which provides that “any person who violates any provision of RA 9344 or any rule or regulation promulgated in accordance thereof shall, upon conviction for each act or omission, be punished by a fine of not less than Twenty Thousand Pesos (P20,000.00) but not more than Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00) or suffer imprisonment of not less than eight (8) years but not more than ten (10) years, or both, such fine and imprisonment at the discretion of the court, unless a higher penalty is provided for in the Revised Penal Code or special laws. If the offender is a public officer or employee, he/she shall, in addition to such fine and/or imprisonment, be held administratively liable and shall suffer the penalty of perpetual absolute disqualification”.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already appropriated in their Annual Budget the 1% for LCPC as per verification of their 2019 annual budget.
observations.reason: The municipality appropriated in their annual budget the 1% for LCPC as per verification of their 2019 annual budget.

observations.recommendation:
We requested for the immediate submission of the required supporting documents with complete data, otherwise the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and shall hold all those who authorized the payments as jointly and severally liable of the amounts disbursed.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials refrain from approving disbursements unless all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim to avoid suspension and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already submitted the required documents. The municipality also complied in their succeeding travel expenses.
observations.reason: The municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We requested for the immediate submission of the required supporting documents with complete data, otherwise the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and shall hold all those who authorized the payment as jointly and severally liable of the amount disbursed. The following documents are as follows:
 Justification why Notice of Award/Notice to Proceed were not posted and why other fields in the Philgeps website were left blank
 Printed Copy of the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed as uploaded in the Philgeps website, if there is any
We recommended that the BAC Officials fill out the required information in the Philgeps website in order for the interested stakeholder to know the whole procurement process. Moreover, such should be updated in accordance with the stage in the procurement process, so that Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed can be posted and updated accordingly, thereby promoting transparency.
Furthermore, we recommend that municipal officials refrain from approving disbursements unless all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim to avoid suspension and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already submitted the required documents and posted the notice of award and notice to proceed in Philgeps.
observations.reason: The municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should:
1. Stop granting honoraria to members of special teams/committees which are performing functions inherent in their regular functions.
2. Cause the refund of honoraria granted to members of the Inspectorate Team after the corresponding Notice of Disallowance shall have become final and executory.
3. Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003, dated October 29, 2012.
observations.managementAction: No update provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency appropriate funds for the titling of the land and the Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort on titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government. The Accounting Office should set up and maintain complete accounting records of real properties and the Municipal Treasurer maintain and keep on file complete records of real properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor’s Office is in the process of titling lots owned by the Municipality. TCTs.
observations.reason: During our exit conference, the municipal officials informed us that 4 lots already have TCTs. The lot titling of the other lots are hold due to boundary disputes between Talisay City and the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all accounts in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445 and to Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-11. To facilitate the timely preparation of the BRS, we also recommended that the Municipality make representation with the depository banks to furnish them Bank Statements within 5 days after the end of each month as provided for in Item 3.1 of the same COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: There was a delay in the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement due to the delayed receipt of monthly Bank Statements. During our exit conference, the Municipal Accountant informed us that they will make representations with the depository banks for the timely receipt of the monthly Bank Statements.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should submit the Trial Balances not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Sec. 70 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. The Municipal Treasurer should submit to the Accountant the Report of Checks Issued/Disbursements and Report of Collections daily together with the Disbursement Vouchers and Official Receipts pursuant to the provisions of the NGAS for LGUs Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The submission of the reports are still not on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property/special education tax collection. The Municipality should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.
observations.managementAction: No update from the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials should prioritize the implementation of projects funded out of Financial Assistance so that expected benefits due to intended recipients be timely achieved or delivered.
observations.managementAction: No update from the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We requested for the submission of reports/documents on the monitoring and assessment made by the Municipality with regards to the performance of the project. We also request the submission of reports on the course of action taken, if any, to address the deficiencies/problems hindering the success of the project.
We recommended that the Municipality continuously monitor the implementation/performance of the project to ensure proper utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The traffic signal is only functioning well during night time.
observations.reason: The officials concerned said that they will undertake the actions as regards the encountered problems.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 5.3 of the NDRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 in the staffing of the Municipality’s LDRRM Office for proper accountability and in order for it to effectively fulfill and achieve its mandate and objectives.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already had the staff assigned at the DRRMO but the said personnel are not yet eligible.
observations.reason: The personnel at the DRRMO are yet to pass the Civil Service Eligibility Exams before they will be given regular positions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that municipal lots are registered under the Torrens Title System to better protect the interest of the municipal government. Proper reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Assessor’s Office should also be done. We also recommended that the Assessor’s Office monitor regularly the status and the purpose of these lots. Additional income could have been earned by the Municipality had these lots been utilized efficiently as sources of revenue in line with the fiscal autonomy of LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor’s Office is in the process of titling lots owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: During our exit conference, the municipal officials informed us that the delay in the titling of land was due to tax/penalty with the BIR and the boundary dispute between Talisay City and Minglanilla.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure that municipal lots are
registered under the Torrens Title System to better protect the interest of the
municipal government. Proper reconciliation between the records of the Municipal
Accountant and the Assessor’s Office should also be done. We also recommend
that the Assessor’s Office monitor regularly the status and the purpose of these lots.
Additional income could have been earned by the Municipality had these lots been
utilized efficiently as sources of revenue in line with the fiscal autonomy of LGU.

We recommend that the Municipality continuously monitor the
implementation/performance of the project to ensure proper utilization of the 20%
Development Fund.

vis-
work schedule or performance standards. SB Members/Municipals
Officials/Resource Persons who were present during the Regular/Special Sessions should
have signed such Minutes to ensure that what is embodied in the Minutes is what was duly
agreed upon. Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all
disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation before payments are made.

We recommend strict compliance with the aforementioned laws and regulations in
the disbursement of government funds for the payment of honoraria and RATA. The
concerned municipal officials should refrain from approving disbursements unless duly
supported with complete documentation. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
should see to it that all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim before

We recommend that extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses incurred by the
Municipality be properly and completely supported with documentary requirements in

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials refrain from approving
disbursements unless all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim to avoid

We recommend that the BAC Officials fill out the required information in the
Philgeps website in order for the interested stakeholder to know the whole procurement
process. Moreover, such should be updated in accordance with the stage in the
procurement process, so that Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed can be posted and

We recommend strict compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 5.3 of
the NDRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 in the staffing of
the Municipality’s LDRRM Office for proper accountability and in order for it to

We recommend that the Municipality appropriate in their Annual Budget one
percent (1%) of its IRA for the implementation/strengthening of programs, projects and

observations.managementAction: Management did not inform us of the action taken on this recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality revisit  the  contract  and  ensure
compliance with the provisions of the contract in line  with  the  Efficient
Solid  Waste  Collection  and  Disposal  System  of  the  Ecological   Waste
Management  Act  of  2000.  Moreover,  there  should  be  proper   planning,
monitoring and implementation of the  Solid  Waste  Management  Plan  taking
into consideration the 20-year  Service  Agreement.  To  ensure  compliance,
relevant ordinances have to be enacted and implemented.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Government  utilize  such  funds  in
accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1,  Allocation  and
Utilization of  the  Local  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and  Management  Fund
(LDRRMF), which provides for the specific guidelines  on  the  projects  and
activities  for  the  utilization  of  the  LDRRMF  pertaining  to  Disaster
Prevention and Mitigation,  Disaster  Preparedness,  Disaster  Response  and
Disaster Rehabilitation and Recovery.



      We recommend that  the Municipality  comply  with  RA  10121  through
effective and efficient use  of  the  LDRRM  Fund  in  accordance  with  the
Implementing Guidelines on the allowed projects and activities  in  relation
to Disaster  Prevention  and  Mitigation,  Disaster  Preparedness,  Disaster
Response and Disaster Rehabilitation and Recovery.  For such to be  properly
implemented, we further recommend the  establishment  of  the  LDRRM  Office
with the required personnel.

We recommend that management immediately stop the practice of allowing
Job Order personnel to perform collecting functions as they are  not  bonded
specifically because an employee-employer relationship between them and  the
municipality does not even exist.


      Strict adherence to Sections 63, 64  and  68  of  PD  1445  is  hereby
recommended.  Only the Municipal Treasurer and  duly  designated  collecting
officers, who are  holders  of  permanent  positions  and  properly  bonded,
should be allowed to perform collecting functions.

We recommend strict compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2015-002 dated March 9, 2015 and 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.  The Accountant should immediately submit the year-end financial statements and supporting schedules.
observations.managementAction: Management have already submitted the CY 2015 FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately implement the identified projects so that expected benefits for the intended beneficiaries/recipients be delivered or achieved.
observations.managementAction: The identified projects have already been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: (i) stop the charging of the payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF; (ii) make proper representation for the payment of the services to the GF; and (iii) ensure that the Special Education Fund be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Management has stopped the practice of charging the payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF and that the Special Education Fund will be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998.

Stop granting honoraria to members of special teams/committees which are performing functions inherent in their regular functions.

3. Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003, dated October 29, 2012.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should support the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables with duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year, in compliance with Section 20, Vol. I of NGAS Manual, to ensure accurate and reliable recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer could not support the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables with duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year due to man power constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel has enough to support for their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they are trying their best to implement our recommendation.
